bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions on people who make a deal with the Devil in German literature. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer some questions on people who make a deal with the Devil in German literature. For 10 points each:
parts: Faust agrees to become Mephistopheles’s servant in exchange for a moment that makes him say “Ah, stay a while! You are so lovely” in Faust, a two-part play by this author of The Sorrows of Young Werther(“VER-ter”).

This author wrote a novella about Peter Schlemihl (“SHLAY-meel”) who sells his soul to the Devil for a bottomless wallet. This French emigré also names a now-defunct literary prize for authors who write in German despite it not being their mother tongue.

Peter Schlemihl appears in The New Year's Eve Adventure, a story by this friend of Adelbert von Chamisso. Nathaniel suspects Coppola is a devilish figure who steals the eyes of children in another story by this author.
